author: dr.smith
content: Patient presents for routine follow-up of hypertension and type 2 diabetes. BP 142/88 mm Hg today — still above JNC 8 target of 140/90 for diabetic patients. Current regimen: lisinopril 10mg daily, metformin 1000mg BID. HbA1c from last month: 7.4% — above ADA target of <7%. Patient reports adherence. Discussed dietary sodium restriction and increased physical activity. Plan: uptitrate lisinopril to 20mg, recheck HbA1c and BMP in 3 months. Continue SMBG twice daily.

author: dr.smith
content: Laboratory results reviewed. HbA1c 7.4% (target <7%). Creatinine 1.1 mg/dL (stable, eGFR approximately 68 mL/min — CKD G2 staging). Potassium 4.2 mEq/L — within normal range. CBC: Hgb 13.8 g/dL (no anaemia). Glucose 118 mg/dL fasting. Overall metabolic control suboptimal but no acute concerns. Continue current medications with uptitration of lisinopril per outpatient plan.

author: dr.jones
content: Patient is a 67-year-old male presenting with exertional dyspnoea and fatigue for 3 weeks. Heart rate 94 /min at rest, irregular rhythm — atrial fibrillation on 12-lead ECG. SpO2 96% on room air. RR 18 /min. No signs of decompensated heart failure at this time. Echocardiogram ordered. Rate control with metoprolol succinate 25mg daily initiated. CHA₂DS₂-VASc score 3 (age 67, male, hypertension) — anticoagulation indicated. Apixaban 5mg BID prescribed. INR monitoring not required with DOAC.

author: dr.jones
content: Cardiology referral for new-onset AF in a 67-year-old male with hypertension. Echo: LVEF 55%, mild LA enlargement (4.4 cm), no valvular disease. Thyroid function normal. Rate control adequate on metoprolol 25mg — resting HR 78 /min at today's visit (AHA lenient target <110 achieved). Rhythm control discussion deferred to cardiology. Anticoagulation with apixaban 5mg BID ongoing. Follow-up in 4 weeks or sooner if symptoms worsen.
